sentence: Hoje o tempo não está nada bom.
ipa: ˈɔʒɨ ˈo ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Inherited pt-PT reduction and coda chiado [ʃ] in 'está'; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Estas uvas custam três euros o quilo.
ipa: ˈeʃtɐʃ ˈyβɐʃ ˈkyʃtɐ̃ ˈtɾez ˈewɾuz ˈo ˈkilu
gloss_en: These grapes cost three euros a kilo.
notes: Stressed /u/ fronted to [y] in 'uvas'/'custam' and intervocalic ⟨v⟩ spirant [β] betacism in 'uvas' per brissos2014 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: A reunião é amanhã às sete da tarde.
ipa: ˈɐ ʁewniˈɐ̃w̃ ˈɛ ɐmɐˈɲã ˈaʃ ˈs̺ɛtɨ ˈdɐ ˈtaɾdɨ
gloss_en: The meeting is tomorrow at seven in the afternoon.
notes: Apico-alveolar [s̺] in 'sete' and palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã'; strong [ʁ] in 'reunião' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: O meu vizinho vive perto da vila.
ipa: ˈo ˈmew biˈz̺iɲu ˈbiβɨ ˈpɛɾtu ˈdɐ ˈbilɐ
gloss_en: My neighbour lives near the village.
notes: Betacism: ⟨v⟩ → [b]/[β] in 'vizinho'/'vive'/'vila', with apical [z̺] in 'vizinho' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: Segue em frente e vira na segunda rua.
ipa: ˈs̺ɨɡɨ ˈẽ ˈfɾẽtɨ ˈe ˈbiɾɐ ˈnɐ s̺ɨˈɡũdɐ ˈʁyɐ
gloss_en: Keep straight ahead and turn on the second street.
notes: Stressed /u/ → [y] in 'rua' after strong [ʁ]; apical [s̺] onset in 'segue'/'segunda' and betacism [b] in 'vira' per brissos2014 and cintra1971.

sentence: A sopa de feijão estava saborosa.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈs̺ɔpɐ ˈdɨ feˈjʒɐ̃w̃ eˈʃtaβɐ s̺ɐbuˈɾɔz̺ɐ
gloss_en: The fish-bean soup was tasty.
notes: Apical [s̺ z̺] in 'sopa'/'saborosa' and 'ei' in 'feijão'; betacism spirant [β] in 'estava' per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Onde é que fica a paragem do autocarro?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈɛ ˈkɨ ˈfikɐ ˈɐ pɐˈɾaʒɨ̃ ˈdu awtuˈkaʁu
gloss_en: Where is the bus stop?
notes: Strong [ʁ] in 'autocarro'; nasal [ɨ̃] in 'paragem' and reduced pretonics per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quantas pessoas vêm ao jantar?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tɐʃ pɨˈs̺ɔɐʒ ˈbẽ ˈɐu ʒɐ̃ˈtaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Betacism ⟨v⟩ → [b] in 'vêm'; apical [s̺] in 'pessoas' and coda [ʃ] in 'quantas' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: Se pudesse, comprava aquela casa.
ipa: ˈs̺ɨ puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾaβɐ ɐˈkɛlɐ ˈkaz̺ɐ
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that house.
notes: Apical [s̺ z̺] in 'pudesse'/'casa'; betacism spirant [β] in 'comprava' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: Comprámos vinho e pão na feira.
ipa: kõˈpɾamuʒ ˈbiɲu ˈe ˈpɐ̃w̃ ˈnɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: We bought wine and bread at the market.
notes: Betacism [b] and palatal [ɲ] in 'vinho'; 'ei' retained in 'feira' per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Amanhã vamos de barco até à barragem.
ipa: ɐmɐˈɲã ˈbamuʒ ˈdɨ ˈbaɾku ɐˈtɛ ˈa bɐˈʁaʒɨ̃
gloss_en: Tomorrow we go by boat to the dam.
notes: Betacism [b] in 'vamos'; strong [ʁ] in 'barragem' and palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: Telefona-me quando chegares a casa.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfɔnɐ ˈmɨ ˈkwɐ̃du ʃɨˈɡaɾɨz ˈɐ ˈkaz̺ɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Apical [z̺] in 'casa'; nasal [ɐ̃] in 'quando' and clitic imperative per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Boa tarde, então como tem passado?
ipa: ˈbɔɐ ˈtaɾd ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkɔmu ˈtẽ pɐˈs̺adu
gloss_en: Good afternoon, so how have you been?
notes: Nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então'; apical [s̺] in 'passado' per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois a voar.
ipa: ˈmajʒ ˈbalɨ ˈum ˈpas̺ɐɾu ˈnɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ ˈkɨ ˈdojz ˈɐ buˈaɾ
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Betacism [b] in 'vale'/'voar'; apical [s̺] in 'pássaro' and coda [ʃ] in 'mais' per cintra1971; proverb attested in cunha_cintra1984.

sentence: O lume já aqueceu tudo.
ipa: ˈo ˈlymɨ ˈʒa ɐkɨˈs̺ew ˈtydu
gloss_en: The fire has already warmed everything.
notes: Stressed /u/ palatalised to [y] in 'lume' [ˈlymɨ] and 'tudo' [ˈtydu] per brissos2014 and cintra1971.

sentence: No sul o número de turistas subiu.
ipa: ˈnu ˈs̺yɫ ˈo ˈnymɨɾu ˈdɨ tuˈɾiʃtɐʃ s̺uˈbiw
gloss_en: In the south the number of tourists rose.
notes: Stressed /u/ → [y] in 'sul' [ˈs̺yɫ] before dark coda [ɫ] and in 'número'; pretonic /u/ in 'turistas' unfronted per brissos2014 and cintra1971.

sentence: O carro do primeiro rapaz ficou no outro lado.
ipa: ˈo ˈkaʁu ˈdu pɾiˈmejɾu ʁɐˈpaʃ fiˈko ˈnu ˈotɾu ˈladu
gloss_en: The first boy's car ended up on the other side.
notes: Strong [ʁ] in 'carro'; 'ei' in 'primeiro' and 'ou' in 'ficou'/'outro' per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O filho da vizinha colheu as maçãs.
ipa: ˈo ˈfiʎu ˈdɐ biˈz̺iɲɐ kuˈʎew ˈɐʒ mɐˈs̺ãʃ
gloss_en: The neighbour's son picked the apples.
notes: Palatals [ʎ] in 'filho' and [ɲ] in 'vizinha'; betacism [b] and apical [z̺] in 'vizinha' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.

sentence: A neta pequena mora na quinta velha.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈnɛtɐ pɨˈkɛnɐ ˈmɔɾɐ ˈnɐ ˈkĩtɐ ˈbɛʎɐ
gloss_en: The little granddaughter lives on the old farm.
notes: Open-mid [ɛ ɔ] in 'neta'/'mora' and palatal [ʎ] in 'velha' with betacism [b] per cintra1971 and mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O sol brilha sobre as colinas.
ipa: ˈo ˈs̺ɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ ˈs̺ɔbɾɨ ˈɐʃ kuˈlinɐʃ
gloss_en: The sun shines over the hills.
notes: Dark coda [ɫ] in 'sol', palatal [ʎ] in 'brilha' and apical [s̺] in 'sol'/'sobre' per cintra1971 and alvarezperez2014.
